Mandela: Long Walk to Freedom (2013)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $8,323,085Details
International Box Office $21,567,317
Worldwide Box Office $29,890,402
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $2,556,966
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,040,255
Total $3,597,221
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Mandela: Long Walk to Freedom celebrates Nelson Mandela's extraordinary journey from his childhood in a rural village through to his election as President of South Africa. It explores the Mandela unknown to most of the world - the lover of fancy cars, a ladies' man, the boxing enthusiast and playboy, the skillful lawyer and freedom fighter. The film is a portrait of the making of a modern icon.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$84,283 (1.0% of total gross)
Legs:3.71 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:27.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$35,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.9 times production budget)
Theater counts:4 opening theaters/1,010 max. theaters, 2.9 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $12,221,804

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: November 29th, 2013 (Limited) by Weinstein Co.
International Releases: November 28th, 2013 (Wide) (South Africa)
December 6th, 2013 (Wide) (Iceland)
December 12th, 2013 (Wide) (Portugal)
December 18th, 2013 (Wide) (Belgium)
December 18th, 2013 (Wide) (France)
Video Release: March 18th, 2014 by Anchor Bay Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for some intense sequences of violence and disturbing images, sexual content and brief strong language.
(Rating bulletin 2286, 8/21/2013)
Running Time: 146 minutes
Plot point: Bigotry, Biography, Government Corruption, Political, Wrongfully Convicted
Subgenre: Biographical Drama
Source:Based on Real Life Events
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Dramatization
Production/Financing Companies: Videovision Entertainment
Production Countries: United Kingdom, South Africa, Canada
Languages: Afrikaans, English, Xhosa

Synopsis

Mandela: Long Walk to Freedom celebrates Nelson Mandela's extraordinary journey from his childhood in a rural village through to his election as President of South Africa. It explores the Mandela unknown to most of the world - the lover of fancy cars, a ladies' man, the boxing enthusiast and playboy, the skillful lawyer and freedom fighter. The film is a portrait of the making of a modern icon.
